# INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.failureRequiringAppLaunch

**Case**

The user must launch your app to request the visual code.

## Declaration

```swift
case failureRequiringAppLaunch
```

## Discussion

Use this response code when you can’t generate the visual code until the user provides additional information in your app. Don’t use this code for general errors or to force the user to launch your app.

## Constants

- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.unspecified**: The response didnt specify a code.
- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.ready**: You’re ready to handle the intent.
- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.continueInApp**: You must launch the app to display the visual code.
- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.inProgress**: You’re in the process of generating the code but aren’t yet finished.
- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.success**: You successfully generated the visual code.
- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.failure**: You were unable to generate the visual code.
- **INGetVisualCodeIntentResponseCode.failureAppConfigurationRequired**: Your app wasn’t configured to display visual codes.
